summary.brief: Hoke v. Miami Valley Hosp.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: In November 2012, on a referral from her gynecologist, Jacqueline Hoke was seen by Debra Miller, M.D., a surgeon board-certified in obstetrics and gynecology and in the sub-specialty of female pelvic medicine and reconstructive surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The April 1, 2013 surgery began with Dr. Miller robotically performing the sacrocolpopexy procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She encountered bleeding. She tied the stitch down to see if what she described in her operation note as 'copious venous bleeding' would resolve. It did not.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Subsequent to the surgery, Mrs. Hoke developed deep vein thrombosis (DVT) and a post-thrombotic syndrome.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Earle M. Pescatore, Jr. is a urogynecologist and a gynecologist practicing in Iowa. Dr. Pescatore testified that it was below the standard of care to fail to identify anatomical 'landmarks' and to have damaged the iliac vein in this surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Earle M. Pescatore, Jr.

evidence_index.text: Dr. Steven McCarus distinguished a surgical 'complication' from a breach of the standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Steven McCarus

evidence_index.text: The jury returned general verdicts for the defendants. For an interrogatory questioning whether they found Dr. Miller to be negligent, they answered 'no.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We affirm the judgment of the Montgomery County Common Pleas Court.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Urinary stress incontinence and symptoms of vaginal prolapse
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient had previous surgery for hysterectomy and anterior vaginal repair of a cystocele in 2009. Symptoms returned, and she complained of urinary stress incontinence.

focused_exam.pelvic: Vaginal vault prolapse, cystocele, rectocele, and urinary stress incontinence diagnosed

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Vaginal vault prolapse with associated cystocele and rectocele

requestables.name: Operation Note
requestables.description: Detailed operation note from the April 1, 2013 surgery

reveal.result_summary: Encountered copious venous bleeding during sacrocolpopexy
reveal.decision_impact: Required additional surgical intervention and consultation

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with symptoms of vaginal prolapse and urinary incontinence

decision_points.prompt: How should the intraoperative bleeding be managed?

options.clinical_reasoning: Immediate consultation could have addressed the bleeding more effectively

options.description: Apply pressure and continue with non-robotic portions of surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Delayed addressing the bleeding, which was not resolved

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant applied pressure and continued with other parts of the surgery
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Pressure was applied to control bleeding while completing other procedures

expected_action_court.description: Immediate consultation with a vascular surgeon should have been sought
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated immediate consultation was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to address bleeding promptly led to complications
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed deep vein thrombosis (DVT) and post-thrombotic syndrome

key_findings: Jury found no negligence by Dr. Miller or MVH
Standard of care was met according to expert testimony

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Not applicable as no breach was found
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no breach was found

factual_timeline.event: Patient seen by Dr. Miller for symptoms of vaginal prolapse

diagnoses.diagnosis: Vaginal vault prolapse, cystocele, rectocele, urinary stress incontinence

complications.complication: Deep vein thrombosis and post-thrombotic syndrome

allegations.allegation: Failure to manage intraoperative bleeding appropriately
Failure to manage surgical bleeding appropriately

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Jury found in favor of defendants
